items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Natural Gas Slides to 16-Month Low Ahead of Cold Shot
items.summary: Spot natural gas prices slumped broadly Tuesday as forecasts pointed to springlike temperatures holding across much of the country, even as colder weather patterns gathered strength.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Spot Gas Prices Slide Despite Incoming Cold Shot
items.summary: Spot gas prices trended lower Tuesday at most major trading hubs even as a new shot of cold air was expected to arrive Wednesday to key consuming regions.

items.title: EIA Lowers Henry Hub Natural Gas Price Forecast Despite Iran War Fallout
items.summary: Lower 48 benchmark natural gas price expectations were revised downward despite recent spikes amid the fog of war in Iran, a new U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) forecast released Tuesday showed.

items.title: Europe Remains Top Destination for U.S. LNG Cargoes Despite Asian Supply Squeeze Amid Middle East War
items.summary: Europe for now remains the most economic destination for U.S. LNG as the incentives to move cargoes to Asia have been reduced since war broke out in the Middle East late last month and curbed the region’s energy exports.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Futures Slide as Warm Weather Offsets Iran War Risk Premium
items.summary: After searching for direction early, April natural gas futures were firmly lower around midday Tuesday as springlike weather and soft demand fueled expectations for modest storage withdrawals ahead of the injection season. Comfortable domestic invent

items.title: Iran War Shakes Global Gas Markets — But Permian Basin Benchmark Waha Still Crashes
items.summary: As some regions of the world panic over an energy squeeze, Perman Basin natural gas is charging into oversupply.

items.title: Heating Demand Drops 21%, Natural Gas Power Burns Slip in Latest Storage Week
items.summary: Natural gas demand weakened during the latest storage week as milder weather slashed heating loads and production ticked higher, setting up a smaller withdrawal in Thursday’s U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) report.

items.title: Enduring Storage Deficits in Key Regions Could Further Bolster Natural Gas Forward Prices
items.summary: While natural gas production is running at robust levels and overall supply in storage is near historical norms, inventories in key gas-consuming regions finished February deep in deficit territory, lending support to forward prices.

items.title: Investors Rotate Into Mid-Cap Energy Names as Big Oil Stalls

items.title: Trump Says It’s “Too Soon” to Talk About Seizing Iran’s Oil
items.summary: Earlier this year, U.S. forces snatched Venezuela’s Nicolas Maduro from Caracas, and the Trump administration stepped in to take over the country’s oil industry. Many appear to have expected the same with Iran, sooner or later, but for now, it seems

items.title: What Can Saudi Arabia Do to Stop Oil Prices From Spiking Further?
items.summary: Saudi Arabia was regarded for decades as the ‘global swing oil producer’, able to quickly decrease or increase its oil flows to respectively raise or lower world oil prices. Riyadh has long perpetuated this idea, with grandiose claims about the Kingd

items.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Sag As Iran War Bolsters Prices
items.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 1.7 million barrels in the week ending March 6, after adding 5.6 million barrels in the week prior. Analysts had expected a build of 1.4 million

items.title: Three Physical Constraints That Will Govern The Price of Oil

items.title: Oil Supply Risks Mount as Iran Lays Mines in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran has begun laying naval mines in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, according to two people familiar with U.S. intelligence reporting, in what analysts warn could extend the effective blockade of the world’s most critical energy chokepoint

items.title: IEA Mulls Emergency Action To Unleash Oil Reserves
items.summary: Global energy leaders are gathering to decide whether to release emergency oil reserves as production and export disruptions tied to the Middle East conflict continue to strain global markets. The International Energy Agency hosted G7 energy minister
